Course Title: AI in Advertising: Truth, Bias, and Manipulation
Category: Media & Entertainment: Publishing, Film, Advertising & Communications, Gaming
Who is this for: Marketing managers, advertising executives, digital marketers, social media managers, brand managers, communications specialists, content creators, media planners, campaign managers
Team or Dept this is for: Marketing, Communications, Digital Marketing, Brand Management, Media, Creative Services, Public Relations
Description: Ethical risks in targeting and messaging.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with how advertising works (such as awareness of online ads, social media marketing, or traditional campaigns) is all you need to get started, as no technical or AI background is required.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key ethical risks associated with AI-driven advertising, explain how algorithmic bias can influence targeting and messaging, and recognise the techniques used to manipulate consumer behaviour. You will also apply practical frameworks to evaluate AI-generated advertising content critically, helping you make more informed and responsible decisions within the advertising and communications industry.
